introSection: RC-48 is a travelling steel roller coaster built by Italian manufacturer Pinfari currently owned by US carnival operator Wade Shows.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride first opened in 2000 on the original pier at Morey's Piers. It closed in 2004, and was later bought by Wade Shows, Inc. During 2008, it was present at the Florida State Fair, Michigan State Fair, Oklahoma State Fair, North Carolina State Fair and perhaps others. For 2009, the colour scheme was changed from silver supports with blue track to blue track and orange supports.

Since 2008, the roller coaster has appeared at the Florida State Fair yearly.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: It was the only RC48 model to be produced by the company.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Information at the Wade Shows website

fullText:
RC-48 is a travelling steel roller coaster built by Italian manufacturer Pinfari currently owned by US carnival operator Wade Shows.

The ride first opened in 2000 on the original pier at Morey's Piers. It closed in 2004, and was later bought by Wade Shows, Inc. During 2008, it was present at the Florida State Fair, Michigan State Fair, Oklahoma State Fair, North Carolina State Fair and perhaps others. For 2009, the colour scheme was changed from silver supports with blue track to blue track and orange supports.

Since 2008, the roller coaster has appeared at the Florida State Fair yearly.

It was the only RC48 model to be produced by the company.






 * Information at the Wade Shows website
